results.possibleIdentity: Thomas L. Eubank (c. 1938 – April 17, 2012) was an industrial designer and technical professional active in New York City during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Industrial Technology from West Virginia State College in 1966. Professionally, Eubank was associated with the firm Aesthetics for Business Incorporated, based in New York, where his work focused on product design, industrial design, and graphics displays. He was a participant in the technical services program of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the arts and engineering. In his later years, he resided in Indianapolis, Indiana, where his passing was marked by a memorial art show, reflecting his lifelong engagement with the intersection of art and design.
